proofs.proof_tex: Proof. Work under the stated hypotheses: ass:margin-window, ass:marginal-overlap, ass:nuisance-rate, the standing feasible-estimator conditions used in oeq:feasible-upper, richness of Pi for the two witness-optimal policies, and gamma <= alpha+1. Let A_alpha=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha). By def:exponents, beta_ag=0 when gamma=0 and beta_ag=alpha gamma/(alpha+1) when gamma>0, D_ag=2+alpha+beta_ag, and r_star(alpha,gamma)=(1+alpha)/D_ag. For the lower bound, thm:minimax-lower applies under the richness hypothesis on Pi and gives, for all large n, M_n(alpha,gamma) >= c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} with c>0 independent of n. This is the left side of the asserted bracket. For the upper bound, take the specific cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM pi_hat_n from def:feasible-erm, with the clip selected as in oeq:feasible-upper. Since M_n is the infimum over all measurable Pi-valued estimators, the risk of this one estimator is an admissible upper witness: M_n(alpha,gamma) <= sup_{P in P_class(alpha,gamma)} E_P R_P(pi_hat_n). The result oeq:feasible-upper bounds the right-hand side by C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma,a,c)} (log n)^p. When gamma>0, the same result identifies the best certified balancing exponent for this plain feasible clipped-AIPW ERM as g(a,c,gamma)=sup_{s>=0} min{A_alpha(1-s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)}, and def:feasible-rate sets r_feas(alpha,gamma,a,c)=min{r_star(alpha,gamma),g(a,c,gamma)}. When gamma=0, ass:strict-overlap-endpoint allows a fixed clip q<=underline_p, beta_ag=0, and def:exponents gives r_star(alpha,0)=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha)=A_alpha; oeq:feasible-upper and def:feasible-rate reduce the feasible exponent to r_feas(alpha,0)=min{A_alpha,c}. Combining these displays proves the two-sided bracket. It remains only to check the phase statements. For gamma>0, r_feas=min{r_star,g}, so r_feas<=r_star. Equality holds if and only if min{r_star,g}=r_star, equivalently g>=r_star. If g<r_star then r_feas=g and the gap r_star-r_feas is strictly positive; conversely a strictly positive gap forces g<r_star. At gamma=0, the identities r_star=A_alpha and r_feas=min{A_alpha,c} give equality if and only if c>=A_alpha=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha), and a strict gap if and only if c<A_alpha. Finally, in the equal-exponent branch, the preceding bracket reads c n^{-r_star} <= M_n <= C n^{-r_star}(log n)^p, so the minimax polynomial exponent is resolved. In the strict-gap branch, the bracket remains c n^{-r_star} <= M_n <= C n^{-g(a,c,gamma)}(log n)^p with g<r_star; deciding whether the true feasible rate equals the converse exponent or the slower feasible exponent is exactly the residual question recorded as oeq:feasible-tight.

open_obligations.what_is_open: In the weak-overlap strict-gap branch gamma>0 with g(a,c,gamma)<r_star(alpha,gamma), determine the sharp welfare-regret exponent attainable by genuinely feasible procedures, including the cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM of def:feasible-erm and possibly other feasible policy learners.
open_obligations.obstruction: The available lower bound thm:minimax-lower is calibrated only to the information needed to distinguish the policy label on the active block and yields the exponent r_star. It does not force the additional weak-arm outcome-regression learning cost that appears in the feasible upper bound through the additive clipped nuisance drift r_mu,n r_e,n/q + r_mu,n q^{1/(2 gamma)}. Conversely, oeq:feasible-upper proves only an achievability result for the declared clipped-AIPW ERM with exponent g in the strict-gap branch; it does not exclude another feasible estimator from attaining r_star.
open_obligations.attempted: Combining the two-point/fuzzy lower construction with the feasible clipped-AIPW upper closes the equal-exponent branch. In the strict-gap branch this combination gives only the honest bracket c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-g(a,c,gamma)}(log n)^p. The existing Le Cam witness cannot by itself prove a slower lower bound, because its Hellinger/KL calibration is already balanced at h_n^{2+alpha+beta_ag} and does not encode first-stage nuisance estimation difficulty.
open_obligations.partial_result: If g(a,c,gamma)>=r_star(alpha,gamma), then r_feas=r_star and thm:rate-characterization gives c n^{-r_star} <= M_n <= C n^{-r_star}(log n)^p, resolving the minimax polynomial exponent up to logarithms. If g(a,c,gamma)<r_star(alpha,gamma), the strongest proved statement is the bracket c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-g(a,c,gamma)}(log n)^p; whether this bracket is tight remains open.
